lines.en_lit: Isn’t there the cubit of the ceiling and the cubit of the plaster the Temple gates, since they were of marble, they constructed them with minimal But isn’t there that minimal since was not cubit he does not consider it

They spread a sheet of fine linen What is different of fine linen? as Rav Kahana said So that he will be aware that the service of the day in fine linen garments. Here, too, so that he will be aware that the service of the day in fine linen garments.

MISHNA: removed descended and immersed. He ascended and dried himself they brought him golden garments and he dressed he sanctified his hands and his feet. They brought him the the daily offering, which he slaughtered And a different completed slaughter on his behalf received the blood and sprinkled it

He entered to burn the morning incense and to remove the lamps and to sacrifice the head and the limbs and the griddle-cake and the wine The morning incense was burned between blood of the limbs; afternoon between limbs libations
if the High Priest was old or delicate [istenis], they heat hot for him and place into the cold in order to temper its chill

GEMARA: The Sages said before Rav Pappa: This not in accordance with Rabbi Meir, as, if Rabbi Meir, since he said: performs two sanctifications upon change of garments, here too, let him perform two sanctifications upon the change of garments.

Rav Pappa said to them: Both to the Rabbis and to Rabbi Meir, one upon removal sacred garments, and one upon donning And here, with regard to this they disagree. “And he shall remove And he shall wash and he shall put on Rabbi Meir holds: juxtaposes removal to donning Just as donning he dons the garments and afterward sanctifies so too, removal he removes and afterward sanctifies
And the Rabbis hold juxtaposes removal to donning Just as donning when he is dressed, he sanctifies so too, removal he sanctifies when he is dressed.

The Sages said to Rav Pappa: And how can you say that Wasn’t it taught They spread a sheet of fine linen between him and the people, and he removed and descended and immersed, he ascended and dried himself. They brought him golden garments, and he donned and he sanctified his hands and his feet. Rabbi Meir says: He removed and sanctified his hands and his feet and descended and immersed. He ascended and dried himself. They brought him golden garments and he donned and sanctified his hands and his feet. He said to them: If it was taught, it was taught.
Granted, Rabbi Meir, that is how you find
